enrichment.summary: Kyle, unable to sleep at 4:30am and newly sober from marijuana, argues that humans do come from monkeys — contra the common scientific hedge that we share a common ancestor. He supports this with a self-taught paleontology framing, the recurrent laryngeal nerve as evidence of fish ancestry, and an ecological argument that environmental niches preserve species forms over time. He connects the hedged language to residual fundamentalist Christian influence and urges people to accept evolutionary lineage straightforwardly.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: We do come from monkeys; the 'common ancestor' framing is misleading and confusing
It proves fish ancestry and how evolution works via incremental building blocks, not intelligent redesign
Stable environments preserve species forms; chimps haven't changed in millions of years for the same reason sharks haven't

stances.topic: Fundamentalist Christian influence on science communication
stances.position: Scientists' reluctance to say 'we come from monkeys' is residual deference to religious pressure
People who reject science (e.g. Trump followers claiming immunity) are being illogical
